reasoning: Use taxtiyi ‘come out’ with the front-of-house phrase kchiki, paralleling 1 where tanu:yi is used with kchiki for entry.
Akpu: marks the roof/chimney area; tuju:yi is ‘go into’ a container/opening; include n chiki ‘(of) the house’.
Akpu: indicates the roof; tukuta is ‘come out (of a hole/chimney)’; followed by n chiki.
Cha:qe: marks the back of the house/door; tanu:yi is ‘enter’; add n chiki.
Lakpa: marks the side of the house; tuju:yi expresses entering a container/opening (window); include n chiki.
Cha:qe: gives the back of the house; tukuta means ‘come out of’ a container (flower box); add n chiki.
